item.title: Tall oil pitch as a graphite feedstock: direct conversion of a biomass-derived by-product into bulk graphite
item.description: Tall oil pitch (TOP), a high-boiling by-product obtained from crude tall oil refining in the kraft pulping process, was converted into bulk graphite through catalyst-free thermal treatment. This result demonstrates that a biomass-derived industrial ...

item.title: Direct detection of reactive intermediates in photocatalytic water splitting with small carbon species on TiO2(100)
item.description: Direct detection of elusive intermediates is crucial for deciphering the mechanisms of photocatalytic reactions at semiconductor interfaces. Time-of-flight mass spectrometry under ultrahigh vacuum conditions in conjunction with ultraviolet laser ...

item.title: Approach-Region Commitment and Environmental Friction
item.description: The commitment of a reacting system to productive barrier crossing depends on how strongly the environment dissipates energy into the approach-region coordinate, rather than oscillatory recrossing. This dissipation is quantified by a single dimensionless ...

item.title: Selectivity Maps for Electrochemical Ethylene Oxidation to Ethylene Oxide and Ethylene Glycol
item.description: Electrochemical ethylene oxidation can produce ethylene oxide and ethylene glycol, but selectivity is limited by competition with the oxygen evolution reaction and overoxidation. At the same time, the mechanistic literature is fragmented across oxo-, ...

item.title: Employing “FAST Water” to Accelerate Self-Assembly
item.description: Molecular dynamics simulations are a powerful tool for elucidating the molecular mechanisms of supramolecular self-assembly, but the long timescales of aggregation often make all-atom simulations computationally challenging. Here, we evaluate the recently ...

item.title: Rapid Evaluation of Glassy Carbon Electrode Surface with Machine Vision
item.description: Automation of electrochemical methods is crucial to achieve high-throughput experimentation (HTE) and self-driving laboratories (SDLs) for accelerating chemistry and materials engineering on pressing research challenges. Obtaining reproducible and ...

item.title: Machine-learning Assisted Raman Spectroscopy for Prediction of Oenological Parameters in Red and White Wines
item.description: Rapid and reliable quantification of oenological parameters is essential for quality control and process optimisation in winemaking. In this study, we investigate the potential of Raman spectroscopy combined with machine learning regression models as a ...

item.title: Nanostructured Hydrogel Surface Functionalization Controls C2C12 Myoblast Adhesion and Differentiation
item.description: Cell culture platforms benefit from chemical cues that recapitulate key aspects of the native biological environment. However, substantial challenges remain in designing scaffolds that also control the spatial organization of these cues. This is ...

item.title: DNA mimic helical foldamers with up to 100 units: synthesis and recognition of a multiprotein complex
item.description: DNA mimic foldamers (DMFOs) are helically folded aromatic oligoamides bearing negatively charged side chains that mimic the shape and charge distribution of double-stranded B-DNA. DNA mimicry typically entails the regular alternation of two different ...

item.title: Process-Informed Co-Optimization of Liquid Air Energy Storage and Data Centers under Off-Design Thermodynamic Operation
item.description: The rapid growth of AI data centers (DCs) is intensifying stress on power systems and exposing operators to volatile electricity markets. This work develops a process-informed co-optimization framework that integrates liquid air energy storage (LAES) with ...

item.title: Sea spray aerosol as a newly identified source of atmospheric gas-phase perfluoroalkyl carboxylic acids
item.description: Perfluoroalkyl carboxylic acids (PFCAs) are persistent environmental contaminants found throughout different ecosystems, including the global oceans. Many of these compounds are bio-accumulative and toxic. Previous studies have shown that sea spray ...

item.title: Machine Learning Reveals Transport-Controlled Performance from a Curated Dataset of Hydrovoltaic Devices
item.description: Hydrovoltaic devices provide an intriguing route for harvesting electricity from water- 14 mediated transport processes, but quantitative comparison across reported systems remains 15

item.title: Hierarchical iDDA-DIA coupled to polarity‑switching MS1 scan samples expands annotation and quantification depth in untargeted metabolomics
item.description: Untargeted LC–HRMS studies face a fundamental trade‑off between MS/MS coverage, MS sampling density, and total analysis time, particularly in large‑scale metabolomics and exposomics projects. To overcome these limitations and enhance both chemical ...

item.title: From binding to bonding: DOSY-NMR to detect covalent ligand-lectin interactions
item.description: Covalent ligands are increasingly exploited in drug discovery yet validating covalent adduct formation typically relies on mass spectrometry and X‑ray crystallography—methods that become resource‑intensive and impractical when extended to the parallel ...

item.title: Playing to Learn: MS Cards, an Educational Card Game for Teaching Mass Spectrometry Instrumentation
item.description: Teaching mass spectrometry presents a significant pedagogical challenge due to its multidisciplinary nature and the complexity of the instrumentation. In an effort to make mass spectrometry learning more engaging and enjoyable for students, a card game ...

item.title: Activity and selectivity descriptors for ethylene glycol electrooxidation from first-principle scaling relations
item.description: The electrooxidation of ethylene glycol emerged as a co-electrolysis anode reaction with the potential of valorizing PET-waste into industrially attractive chemicals such as glycolic acid. Although the focus on the reaction is increasing steadily in ...

item.title: Temperature-and pressure-dependent water stabilization of CO dimerization on Cu(100)
item.description: The electrochemical CO(2) reduction reaction offers a pathway to producing fuels and high-value chemicals from waste CO2 using renewable electricity. Among known catalysts, only copper in aqueous electrolytes enables the formation of C2+ products with ...

item.description: Electrochemical CO2 reduction (eCO2R) offers a route to convert renewable electricity into fuels and chemicals while closing the anthropogenic carbon cycle. However, the technology remains immature and is limited by low selectivity and energy efficiency. ...

item.description: Simultaneous photochemical and photocatalytic degradation of pollutants under visible light presents a fundamental reactor design challenge because both pathways compete for the same incident photon flux. Photons absorbed directly by the pollutant drive ...

item.title: Light drives bidirectional two-electron transfer between paired dynamic redox molecules
item.description: We demonstrate closed photoredox systems in which light alone drives a net two-electron transfer between pairs of dynamic redox (dyrex) switches. The same pair can be cycled between two thermodynamically stable states using light of orthogonal wavelengths,...

item.description: Time series are ubiquitous, from climate science, healthcare to economics. The high dimensionality, complex correlations, and multiscale dynamics in rich time-series data challenge expert intuition and drive the development of machine-learning approaches. ...

item.description: Reaction kinetics is a foundational topic in undergraduate curricula. There is continued interest in developing instructional laboratory experiments that enable students to directly visualize reaction progress and to study reaction kinetics quantitatively ...

item.title: Can catalytic activity be varied on different Cu sites for HCOO hydrogenation?
item.description: HCOO pathway dominates the production of CH3OH synthesis on Cu catalysts. HCOO hydrogenation is therefore of crucial importance for its kinetic hurdle. It is reported catalytic variation on different Cu facets as their surface Cu atoms are of different ...

item.title: FAVES V4: A Production-Deployed Compliance and Liability Screening System for Drug Discovery at 122M-Compound Scale, Aligned to HHS/ONC FAVES and NIST AI 600-1
item.description: FAVES V4 is a compliance and liability screening system for drug discovery, aligned to two complementary federal frameworks: the U.S. Department of Health and Human Services / Office of the National Coordinator FAVES rubric for trustworthy AI in ...

item.title: NovoExpert-2: State-of-the-Art ADMET Prediction via Gradient-Boosted Trees on MapLight Fingerprints and GIN Embeddings
item.description: We present NovoExpert-2, a dual-method approach to molecular property prediction that achieves state-of-the-art performance on five endpoints of the Therapeutics Data Commons (TDC) ADMET benchmark. Our primary model combines MapLight-style fingerprint fea-...

item.title: From 2D Graphs to Sub-Unit pKa: Nine Iterations to Beat MolGpKa on Both Scaffolds and Sulfonamides
item.description: Accurate pKa prediction for drug-like molecules remains a significant gap between com- mercial tools (Epik ∼0.5 RMSE, MoKa ∼0.4 RMSE) and open-source methods, particularly for sulfonamides and heterocyclic N–H compounds prevalent in medicinal chemistry. ...

item.title: Molecular Targets Shape Reasoning Topology: Bidirectional Validation of Chemistry-Inspired Bond Classification for AI Reasoning Chains
item.description: Recent work classified AI reasoning chains using chemistry-inspired bond types, namely covalent (deductive), hydrogen (reflective), and van der Waals (exploratory), showing that structural stability of reasoning predicts training data quality (Direction A)...

item.title: Human NEU3 and NEU4 enzymes prefer short polysialic acid substrates
item.description: Regulation of sialic acids is important to many biological processes and involves both biosynthesis and catabolism. The human neuraminidase enzymes (hNEU) regulate many sialosides in human health and disease. Polysialic acid (polySia) is a polyanion of α(...

item.title: A Machine Learning QSAR Model for Predicting hTERT Telomerase Inhibitor Potency: ChEMBL Dataset Curation, Algorithm Benchmarking, and Prospective Application to Natural and Synthetic Anticancer Compounds
item.description: Human telomerase reverse transcriptase (hTERT) is reactivated in approximately 85–90% of human cancers, establishing it as one of the most broadly expressed oncological drug targets; yet no quantitative structure–activity relationship (QSAR) model trained ...

item.title: RMG-Py v4: 17 Years of Open Source Reaction Mechanism Generation
item.description: Kinetic modeling describes reacting chemical systems through networks of elementary reactions and their associated rate parameters. Reaction Mechanism Generator (RMG-Py) automates the construction of these networks by simulating reaction fluxes to ...

item.title: Evidencing Fast and Reversible Proton Insertion of a Metastable Bilayered Tungsten Oxide
item.description: Materials that exhibit fast and reversible electrochemical modulation of properties such as electronic conductivity, optical absorption, and volume are of interest for electrochromic, neuromorphic, and actuator switching technologies. Here, we report on ...

item.title: The thermodynamic limit of battery degradation predictability is nine orders of magnitude below empirical variance
item.description: Capacity fade in lithium-ion batteries exhibits significant cell-to-cell variance, fundamentally limiting the accuracy of lifetime predictions. Current approaches treat this variance as an intrinsic feature of the electrochemical system and attempt to ...

item.title: Bismuth-Catalyzed Synthesis of Carbon Nanobelts
item.description: Carbon nanobelts (CNBs) have attracted widespread interest, yet their precise synthesis remains a significant challenge. Herein, we report the efficient synthesis of two novel CNBs, namely (18,12) CNB [30] and (18,15) CNB [33], which represent the ...

item.title: Potential‑Driven Tetragonal CuFe2O4 Phase Transition Enables Near‑Complete Nitrate‑to‑Ammonia Conversion with Minimal Nitrite Accumulation
item.description: The development of electrocatalyst for nearly complete electrocatalytic nitrate reduction without accumulating nitrites in the product feed is indispensable for sustainable nitrate contaminated wastewater treatment and carbon-neutral green ammonia ...

item.title: Coordination-Enabled Antibiotic Capture and Optical Sensing in Water with a Dual-Function Mesoporous Zirconium Metal-Organic Framework
item.description: Metal-organic frameworks (MOFs) combining high porosity and structural robustness provide unique platforms for the selective capture of pollutants in water. In addition, they can serve as platforms to immobilize luminescent species for sensing ...

item.title: (Hetero)adamantane synthesis: A triple alkylation reaction
item.description: (Hetero)adamantanes containing tetrel bridgeheads (C, Si, and Ge) are important structures in surface and chemical sciences. Syntheses of diamondoid structures generally occur in a linear fashion, often using harsh conditions. These limitations make it ...

item.title: Physicochemical and Structural Determinants of BACE1-Selective Inhibition: An Integrative Modeling Study using A Combined Ligand-and Structure-Based Approach

item.description: Molecular dynamics (MD) simulations are a powerful computational tool for exploring the behavior of atoms and molecules at atomic resolution. Despite their growing importance across various scientific disciplines, newcomers often find the theoretical and ...

item.title: Chemically Interpretable Explanations for Molecular Property Prediction via Fragment-Level Shapley Values
item.description: Machine learning has emerged as a powerful approach for molecular property prediction and drug discovery. However, the black-box nature of many machine learning models limits their interpretability, trustworthiness, and adoption in interdisciplinary ...

item.title: Networked Open-Source Infrastructure for Scalable Analysis of Chemical Ionization Mass Spectrometry Data
item.description: Chemical Ionization Mass Spectrometry (CIMS) generates large and complex data sets, with sampling cadences that vary widely by instrument configuration—from sub-second to seconds for online deployments, to minutes or hours for filter-based systems. These ...

item.title: Impurity-Activated Corrosion Mechanisms in Molten Halide Salts from Foundation Model Molecular Dynamics
item.description: Molten salt corrosion remains a major challenge in energy applications where molten salts are in direct contact with structural alloys. In particular, experiments find that the impurity level in the molten salt has a substantial influence on the corrosion ...

item.title: Accelerating the deployment of nitrogen diversion and recovery technologies in water resource recovery facilities
item.description: Nitrogen recovery from human excreta is advancing rapidly at the laboratory scale, yet its potential in full-scale water resource recovery facilities (WRRFs) remains unrealized. To expedite the deployment of nitrogen recovery technology across diverse ...

item.title: Stereospecific Engagement of a Methyl-Sensitive ECL2 Anchor Site in 5-HT₂A by α-Substituted NBOH Derivatives

item.description: A family of fluoroborates dyes resembling the topology of pentazine, a molecule reported to have an inverted singlet-triplet energy gap (i.e., -DEST), were designed and synthesised. This combined experimental and quantum chemical study explores their ...

item.title: Integrating Enhanced Molecular Sampling, RNA-Specific Scoring Functions, and SILCS Technology for Small-Molecule Targeting of RNA
item.description: Increasing knowledge of the wide range of biological functions of ribonucleic acids (RNAs) have made them a critical target in drug development. Accordingly, modifying their function through targeting them with small, drug-like molecules for the ...

item.title: Learning from SILCS FragMaps: Combining Physics-Based Energies with Artificial Intelligence for Rapid Drug Permeability Prediction
item.description: Prediction of drug passive permeability represents an important component of rational drug design requiring continued development of methods to address this problem. Artificial intelligence (AI) methods have long been shown to be of utility for prediction ...

item.title: Synthesis, Redox Properties and Magneto-Optical Activity of a Homologous Set of Metallo-5,5-Dimethylisocorrole Complexes
item.description: A homologous set of 5,5-dimethyl-10,15-bis(pentafluorophenyl)-isocorrole (5-DMIC) complexes supporting NiII, PdII, PtII, or ZnII metal centers have been prepared and characterized. These complexes are readily accessible via modular synthetic routes and ...

item.description: Generative models could accelerate fluorophore discovery, but wavelength conditioning is difficult when red and near-infrared emitters occupy sparse, family-structured regions of chemical space. We introduce FluoFlow, a fragment-tree flow-matching ...

item.title: Utility of Acid Co-Product from Bipolar Membrane Electrodialysis as a Function of System-scale, Location, and Flexible Operation
item.description: Bipolar membrane electrodialysis (BPMED) produces acid and base from seawater, and is being primarily advanced for base generation, with co-produced acid needing valorization. Acid cost and utility depend on process scale, electricity prices, and ...

item.title: Parallel Evolution of Dirhodium Artificial Metalloenzymes Creates Distinct Reaction Environments for Selective Carbene Transfer
item.description: Artificial metalloenzymes (ArMs) combine the reactivity of transition metal catalysts with the selectivity and evolvability of protein scaffolds. Understanding different mechanisms by which scaffolds can impart selectivity would greatly facilitate ArM ...

item.title: Continuous Flow Electrophotochemical Minisci Reaction via Catalytic Benzophenone Regeneration
item.description: The use of benzophenone as a hydrogen atom transfer (HAT) photocatalyst is limited by catalyst deactivation through ketyl radical dimerization, often requiring stoichiometric loadings. Herein, we report a continuous flow electrophotochemical strategy that ...

item.title: On again, off again: thiol-Michael photoactivated dynamic covalent chemistry
item.description: Light-responsive dynamic covalent chemistry is a powerful tool that can provide spatiotemporal control of dynamic bond exchange when coupled with light as an external stimulus. Despite its promise, there is a need to develop new tools to harness light for ...

item.title: Extending Generative Machine Learning for Sustainable Polymer Design: An Integrated Workflow from Generative Models to Bio-Based Candidates
item.description: The discovery of sustainable bio-based polymers is a pressing need, as traditional synthetic polymers derived from fossil fuels pose significant environmental challenges. However, bio-based alternatives often occupy different regions of chemical space and ...

item.title: Impact of Chronic Hydration–Dehydration Cycling on Enamel Analog Microhardness and Crack Propagation: An in Vitro Model of Dry Environmental Stress
item.description: Objective: To evaluate the effects of repeated hydration–dehydration cycles on the mechanical integrity of enamel analog materials, focusing on microhardness reduction and crack propagation behavior under simulated dry conditions. Methods: Commercial ...

item.title: Fluorescent Metal–Organic Frameworks for Chemical Sensing: Design Strategies, Mechanisms, and Analytical Applications
item.description: Fluorescent metal-organic frameworks (MOFs) have emerged as transformative platforms for chemical sensing, combining ultrahigh porosity, tunable optical properties, and versatile host-guest chemistry to achieve exceptional sensitivity and selectivity. ...

item.title: The Key Role of Solvation in Enhancing Electrochemical Promotion of Brønsted Acid Catalysis
item.description: Electrostatic polarization of catalyst-solution interfaces has been demonstrated to modulate the rate of liquid-phase heterogeneous Brønsted acid catalysis by up to five orders of magnitude. Previous studies in acetonitrile establish that maximal rate-...

item.title: All-Photonic Yellow-Red-Blue-Green Orthogonal Photoswitching for Rewritable Four-Color 2D and 3D Displays and Optical Memories
item.description: Light-induced color change is the defining and most fundamental characteristic of a photochromic material. Twofold switching between colorless and color or two different colors is known to humanity for centuries and has been used allegedly already in ...

item.title: Interpretable machine learning reveals physicochemical drivers of bio-crude yield in hydrothermal liquefaction of water hyacinth
item.description: Hydrothermal liquefaction (HTL) is a promising pathway for converting wet biomass into biocrude oil; however, the complex interactions among process parameters limit mechanistic understanding and optimization. In this study, an interpretable machine ...

item.title: Descriptor Adequacy as a Materials-Regime Principle in MOF Adsorption Machine Learning
item.description: Machine learning is changing how metal--organic frameworks (MOFs) are screened for gas storage and separation, but its reliability depends on a basic question: does the descriptor contain the physics that controls the adsorption property? Here we show ...

item.title: Excited Triplet States during Organofluorine Molecular Dynamics upon Electron-Impact—A Study of Electronic Structure and Molecular Fragmentation
item.description: The semiconductor industry’s reliance on high global warming potential (GWP) perfluorocarbon etchants motivates the search for environmentally sustainable alternatives. Although exchange scattering populates triplet states efficiently during electron-...

item.title: Guiding photochemical process intensification through kinetic diagnostics
item.description: Photochemical methodologies are commonly optimized towards yield, yet yield alone provides limited insight into productivity and scalability. Here, we demonstrate how simple kinetic diagnostics can guide photochemical process intensification by ...

item.title: In Silico Drug Discovery of Novel Fluorinated Phenylpropanoic Acid Derivatives as Tubulin Colchicine Binding Site Inhibitors: A Systematic Lead Optimization Study
item.description: Tubulin, a key structural protein involved in mitotic spindle formation, represents a validated anticancer target. The Colchicine Binding Site (CBS) on the beta-tubulin subunit offers a promising avenue for the development of novel antimitotic agents. In ...

item.title: Simple and reproducible ready-to-use TMB solution for enzyme-linked immunosorbent assay
item.description: The enzyme-linked immunosorbent assay (ELISA) is a cornerstone of modern laboratory diagnostics, yet its routine implementation is often constrained by the cost and logistics of chromogenic substrates. While commercial 3,3',5,5'-tetramethylbenzidine (TMB) ...

item.title: Influence of the surface Rock-Salt Layer in Ni-rich NMC cathodes
item.description: Rock-Salt Layer (RSL) formation is one of the main degradation processes in Nirich cathodes for Li-ion batteries, giving rise to loss of capacity and cyclability. Our combined theory and experimental study examines in detail the impact of different ...

item.title: Varying Mechanisms Dominate Formate Formation in Electrochemical CO2 Reduction on Cu, Au and Pb: A Constant Potential DFT-based Microkinetic Analysis
item.description: Electrochemical CO2 reduction (eCO2R) offers a route to convert renewable electricity into fuels and chemicals while closing the anthropogenic carbon cycle. However, the technology remains immature and is limited by low selectivity and energy efficiency. ...

item.title: Boundaries of the Hammett Equation: Critical Window and Defenestration Window in CMS

item.description: Automated laboratories increasingly combine automated and cloudified instruments, robotic operation, sample provenance and data infrastructures, and autonomous agents. Yet these capabilities primarily address execution, while planning remain limited to ...

item.title: Accurate 20 ms protein sizing with microscope‑based dynamic light scattering
item.description: Dynamic Light Scattering (DLS) is a powerful technique to probe nano to microscale objects in solution. Among its main limitations is the requirement of steady-state conditions during measurement, restricting its applicability towards time evolving ...

item.title: Visible range sub band gap photoexcitation of conductivehydrogenated nanodiamonds
item.description: Hydrogenated milled nanodiamonds (H-MND) are emerging as original candidates for photocatalysis applications, demonstrating notably outstanding performances for PFOA degradation in water under solar-type illumination. However, the involved sub-band gap ...

item.title: Mobility Suppression Boundary Principle for Polymer Glass-Transition Temperature
item.description: Polymer glass-transition temperature (Tg) varies widely even among repeat units with similar visible chemistry and size. This manuscript formulates and tests the Mobility Suppression Boundary Principle (MSBP-Tg): within comparable chemistry-size fibers, ...

item.title: Tall oil pitch as a graphite feedstock: direct conversion of a biomassderived by-product into bulk graphite
item.description: Tall oil pitch (TOP), a high-boiling by-product obtained from crude tall oil refining in the kraft pulping process, was converted into bulk graphite through catalyst-free thermal treatment. This result demonstrates that a biomass-derived industrial ...

item.title: Elucidating mobile charge carriers in polymeric carbon nitrides by transient surface photovoltage analysis
item.description: Understanding charge carrier dynamics in granular photocatalysts is critical for rational materials design, yet conventional spectroscopies lack the spatial directionality needed to distinguish individual electron and hole contributions. Here, time-...

item.title: Observation of ”negative” persistent photoconductivity effect at T > 200 oC in N-polar GaN/AlGaN/GaN heterostructures
item.description: It is rarely reported that semiconductor materials can exhibit the ”negative” persistent photoconductivity (PC) effect. In this work the ”negative” persistent PC effect was observed in the Nitrogen (N)-polar oriented GaN/AlGaN/GaN heterostructure under ...
